topics.title: Emerging AI Startups
topics.summary:
Based on my search results, here are the key news stories about emerging AI startups from the last 24 hours:

## Key News About Emerging AI Startups (Last 24 Hours)

### 1. **CB Insights AI 100 List Released**


CB Insights announced its tenth annual AI 100, showcasing the 100 most promising private artificial intelligence companies in the world

. 

This year's cohort spans autonomous security operations, humanoid robots, and domain-specific AI for healthcare and financial services

. Notable companies named to the list include Rhino Federated Computing, Alex AI (the first recruiting software company on the list), Straiker (AI security), and 7AI (security operations).

### 2. **Sierra Raises $950M at $15B Valuation**


Eight months after closing a $350 million funding round, Sierra Technologies Inc. announced that it has raised an additional $950 million at a $15 billion valuation

. 

Alphabet Inc.'s GV venture capital arm and Tiger Global led the investment

. 

According to Sierra, its software has been adopted by nearly half the Fortune 50 and generates $150 million in annual recurring revenue

.

### 3. **Record AI Startup Funding in Q1 2026**


AI shattered records last quarter, with $242 billion — 80% of total global venture funding in Q1— going to companies in the sector

. 

Crunchbase data shows investors poured $300 billion into 6,000 startups globally in the quarter, up over 150% quarter over quarter and year over year

.

### 4. **AI Security Startups Gaining Prominence**


7AI was one of only six security companies named to the cross-industry list, underscoring the company's market-leading role in advancing agentic AI from concept to production inside one of the enterprise's most demanding environments: the security operations center

. This highlights growing investment in AI security solutions.

### 5. **Emerging Startups Show Real Traction**


The AI 100 companies raised $10.9B in equity funding raised over time, including more than $2B in 2026 so far

. The emphasis is on companies demonstrating "proof of real traction outside a demo environment" across sectors including healthcare, financial services, and enterprise software.

**Sources:** CB Insights, SiliconANGLE, CNBC, Crunchbase News, PRNewswire, Globe Newswire

topics.title: Latest AI news
topics.summary:
Let me search for more specific recent AI news stories from the last 24 hours.

Let me search for one more specific recent development to ensure I have the most current information.

Based on my search results, here are the most important AI news developments from the last 24 hours:

## Key AI News (Last 24 Hours - May 6, 2026)

### 1. **OpenAI Reveals Low-Latency Voice AI Infrastructure**


OpenAI published details on May 4, 2026 about how it delivers low-latency voice AI at scale, explaining that "voice AI only feels natural if conversation moves at the speed of speech."

 

The system now serves more than 900 million weekly active users with fast connection setup and low media round-trip time.

 This infrastructure powers ChatGPT voice and the Realtime API.

**Source:** OpenAI Engineering Blog

### 2. **Pentagon Partners with 8 Major Tech Companies for AI**


The Department of Defense announced on May 1, 2026 an agreement with eight major technology companies to use their AI tools in classified networks, including SpaceX, OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon Web Services, Oracle and Reflection.

 

Notably excluded was Anthropic, after President Trump severed ties with the company over its refusal to allow military use of Claude for "all lawful purposes," including autonomous weapons, with the Pentagon declaring Anthropic a "supply chain risk."



**Source:** CNN Business

### 3. **AI Cybersecurity Threats Accelerating**


Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report found that time-to-exploit has effectively gone negative, with exploits now routinely arriving before patches, and 28.3% of CVEs exploited within 24 hours of disclosure.

 

The UK's AISI (AI Safety Institute) reports that frontier cyber-offense capabilities are doubling every four months.



**Source:** The Hacker News, Air Street Press

### 4. **Chinese AI Labs Release Competitive Open-Weight Models**


Four Chinese labs released open-weights coding models within a 12-day window: Z.ai's GLM-5.1, MiniMax M2.7, Moonshot's Kimi K2.6, and DeepSeek V4, all reaching similar capability levels to Western frontier models at significantly lower inference costs.

 

None costs more than a third of Claude Opus 4.7.



**Source:** Air Street Press

### 5. **Anthropic Expands Financial Sector AI Agents**


Anthropic released ten preconfigured AI agents for the financial sector designed to automate typical tasks performed by investment banks, asset managers and insurers.

 

According to CNN Business, Anthropic now wins about 70% of head-to-head matchups against OpenAI among businesses purchasing AI services for the first time.



**Source:** LLM Stats, TLDL AI News

These developments highlight the rapid maturation of AI infrastructure, increasing geopolitical tensions around AI governance, growing cybersecurity threats from AI-assisted attacks, and intensifying competition in the AI model marketplace.

topics.title: New AI models
topics.summary:
Based on my search results, I found limited specific news from the last 24 hours. The most recent information is from May 5-6, 2026. Here's a summary of the most important recent news about new AI models:

## Key News About New AI Models (Last 24-48 Hours)

### 1. **GPT-5.5 Matches Cybersecurity Capabilities**


OpenAI's newly public GPT-5.5 matched Anthropic's restricted Mythos Preview model on cybersecurity challenges, scoring 71.4% on the hardest "Expert" tasks versus Mythos's 68.6%, and built a disassembler for a Rust binary autonomously in 10 minutes at a cost of $1.73

. This development occurred around May 5, 2026.

**Source:** Radical Data Science

### 2. **Anthropic's New Tokenizer for Claude Opus 4.7**


Anthropic improved Claude Opus 4.7's understanding of inputs with a new tokenizer

, announced on May 5, 2026.

**Source:** Radical Data Science

### 3. **IBM's "Bob" AI Coding Assistant**


IBM released "Bob," a friendly AI coding assistant

, announced on May 6, 2026.

**Source:** AI Business

### 4. **Chinese Labs Release Wave of Open-Weight Coding Models**


Four Chinese labs released open-weights coding models within a 12-day window: Z.ai's GLM-5.1, MiniMax M2.7, Moonshot's Kimi K2.6, and DeepSeek V4, all achieving agentic engineering capabilities at less than a third the cost of Claude Opus 4.7

.

**Source:** Air Street Press (State of AI: May 2026)

### 5. **Anthropic Releases Financial Sector AI Agents**


Anthropic released ten preconfigured AI agents for the financial sector designed to automate typical tasks performed by investment banks, asset managers and insurers

.

**Source:** LLM Stats / Bloomberg

These developments highlight the rapid advancement in AI model capabilities, particularly in cybersecurity, coding assistance, and specialized vertical applications.

topics.title: New announcements from leading tech companies
topics.summary:
Let me search for more specific recent announcements:

Let me search for more recent news from the last 24 hours:

Based on my search results, here's a summary of the most important tech announcements from the last 24 hours:

## Key Tech News from the Last 24 Hours (May 6, 2026)

### 1. **OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5 Instant as New Default Model**


OpenAI is deploying GPT-5.5 Instant to all users starting today, with the model able to use its search tool to refer back to past conversations, files, and Gmail

. 

This feature will be available to Plus and Pro users on the web, with plans to roll it out to mobile soon

. This follows the company's GPT-5.5 release from mid-April 2026.

**Source:** Engadget, TechCrunch

### 2. **Pentagon Strikes AI Deals with 8 Major Tech Companies**


The companies involved in the deal include Elon Musk's SpaceX, ChatGPT-maker OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon Web Services, Oracle and Reflection

. 

Not included: Anthropic, which the Trump administration has blacklisted over Anthropic's insistence that the Pentagon include certain safety guardrails for the government's use of AI in warfare

.

**Source:** CNN Business

### 3. **AWS Announces What's Next with AWS 2026 Event & OpenAI Partnership**


OpenAI models on Amazon Bedrock (Limited preview) brings the latest OpenAI models, including GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.4, to the Bedrock APIs

. 

Amazon Quick expands with a desktop app, new pricing plans, and visual asset generation

.

**Source:** AWS Blog

### 4. **Big Tech Earnings Show Massive AI Investment Results**


Meta's revenue jumped 33%, Alphabet's Google Cloud revenue soared 63% year over year, and Amazon's cloud computing business grew at its highest rate in 15 quarters

. 

Management reaffirmed plans to introduce ads in Apple Maps in the U.S. and Canada this summer

.

**Source:** The Motley Fool, CNBC

### 5. **Sam Altman Confirms GPT-5.5 Planned Its Own Launch Party**


OpenAI CEO Sam Altman confirms GPT-5.5 will host its own launch party on May 5 at 5:55pm, showcasing unusual self-referential AI behavior

. 

The AI chose May 5 at 5:55pm, requested that its human creators deliver a toast instead of speaking itself, and asked for a dedicated station where guests could submit ideas for GPT-5.6

.

**Source:** Technobezz

These announcements highlight the rapid pace of AI development, increasing government partnerships with tech companies, and major cloud infrastructure growth across the industry.
